message.content:
You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_24.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 24: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[24]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_24.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 24: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""TURNKEY small-scale validation probe (I-deepfix-001 #1344).
3	
4	Proves the A5 BREADTH fix (the §-1.3 demote-not-drop + raised fetch budget) and the
5	A6 KIMI-JUDGE swap (qwen 429-tear -> moonshotai/kimi-k2.6) CHEAPLY on the VM, WITHOUT
6	the full $40 8-query sweep. Two probes:
7	
8	  * PROBE 1 (breadth): a single NON-simple ``--only`` query run on the VM. The §-1.3
9	    demote + fetch-budget proof lives in the FRONT-HALF manifest fields, so a single
10	    cheap query is enough -- the proof is the MECHANISM firing, not scale.
11	  * PROBE 2 (kimi): a ``--resume`` from a SMALL banked corpus_snapshot, which re-runs
12	    the 4-role D8 seam with the default kimi judge (no re-fetch -> cheap). This is the
13	    LIVE companion to the diced preflight's ``D4_four_role_judge_seam_LIVE`` dice.
14	
15	THIS RUNNER NEVER SPENDS. It has exactly two modes:
16	
17	  * PRINT mode (no ``--assert-dir``): PRINTS the exact VM command(s) the OPERATOR runs.
18	    It launches nothing, loads no model, calls no OpenRouter endpoint.
19	  * ASSERT mode (``--assert-dir DIR``): runs OFFLINE, read-only assertions on an
20	    ALREADY-PRODUCED run_dir. It only reads JSON/JSONL the run already wrote and shells
21	    out to the (offline, read-only) diced preflight. No paid call, no model load.
22	
23	Per CLAUDE.md memory (ALL heavy GPU+LLM runs on the VM, never local; §8.4): the actual
24	VM runs are the OPERATOR's to launch. This harness is the BUILD + the offline-assert glue.
25	
26	WHY NO ``--stop-after corpus_snapshot``: ``scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py`` has NO
27	stop-after / front-half-only flag (traced 2026-06-30: argparse exposes only --only,
28	--out-root, --resume, --pathB-gate, --replay-from-pin). The ``corpus_snapshot.json``
29	checkpoint persists DATA + count subsets but NOT ``retrieval.relevance_gate`` (the §-1.3
30	demote proof lives ONLY in the finalized ``manifest.json``). So the cheapest HONEST
31	front-half breadth proof is a single NON-simple ``--only`` query run end-to-end -- far
32	cheaper than the $40 8-query sweep, and its front-half breadth fields are present in the
33	manifest regardless of the back half. (Do NOT pick a trivially-simple query: the sweep's
34	simple-router drops the fetch budget to ``PG_SIMPLE_FETCH_CAP``=40, which would defeat the
35	"fetched materially > legacy 40" proof. A drb_* benchmark slug is non-simple by routing.)
36	
37	LAW VI: every host/path/threshold is an env var (``PG_DICED_*`` / ``PG_PROBE_*``).
38	LAW II / §8.4: no network, no model load, no heavy src import. Pure stdlib; this module
39	NEVER imports ``src.polaris_graph`` so the heavy package __init__ never runs.
40	
41	Usage
42	-----
43	    python scripts/small_scale_validation_probe.py --probe breadth     # print the VM cmd
44	    python scripts/small_scale_validation_probe.py --probe kimi         # print the VM cmd
45	    python scripts/small_scale_validation_probe.py --probe both         # print both cmds
46	    python scripts/small_scale_validation_probe.py --probe breadth --assert-dir DIR
47	    python scripts/small_scale_validation_probe.py --probe kimi    --assert-dir DIR
48	
49	Exit code: 0 == all selected checks GREEN (or PRINT mode), 1 == >=1 check RED (fail-loud),
50	2 == harness error (e.g. --assert-dir not found).
51	"""
52	
53	from __future__ import annotations
54	
55	import argparse
56	import json
57	import os
58	import subprocess
59	import sys
60	import tempfile
61	from dataclasses import dataclass
62	from pathlib import Path
63	from typing import Any, Optional
64	
65	_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
66	_DEFAULT_DICED_PREFLIGHT = _REPO_ROOT / "scripts" / "pipeline_diced_preflight.py"
67	
68	# Manifest hard-fail status the kimi seam must NOT reach.
69	_TRANSPORT_EXHAUSTED = "abort_role_transport_exhausted"
70	# The disclosed-gap label the kimi seam must NOT emit on a clean completion.
71	_SEAM_UNADJUDICATED = "four_role_seam_unadjudicated"
72	
73	GREEN = "GREEN"
74	RED = "RED"
75	
76	
77	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
78	# Env helpers (LAW VI)
79	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
80	
81	def _env(name: str, default: str) -> str:
82	    raw = os.environ.get(name)
83	    return default if raw is None or raw.strip() == "" else raw
84	
85	
86	def _env_int(name: str, default: int) -> int:
87	    raw = os.environ.get(name)
88	    if raw is None or raw.strip() == "":
89	        return default
90	    return int(raw)
91	
92	
93	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
94	# Check primitive
95	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
96	
97	@dataclass
98	class Check:
99	    name: str
100	    passed: bool
101	    detail: str
102	
103	    @property
104	    def status(self) -> str:
105	        return GREEN if self.passed else RED
106	
107	
108	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
109	# VM command builders (placeholders consistent with pipeline_diced_preflight.py: PG_DICED_VM_*)
110	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	
112	def _ssh_prefix() -> str:
113	    """The ssh destination, key, and port -- all env-overridable, defaults from the campaign VM
114	    (memory: VM == ssh2.vast.ai:37450). ``PG_DICED_VM_HOST`` is the SAME env name the diced
115	    preflight reads, so the two harnesses target the same box by default."""
116	    key = _env("PG_DICED_VM_KEY", "~/.ssh/id_ed25519")
117	    port = _env("PG_DICED_VM_PORT", "37450")
118	    host = _env("PG_DICED_VM_HOST", "root@ssh2.vast.ai")
119	    return f"ssh -i {key} -p {port} {host}"
120	
121	
122	def _remote_repo() -> str:
123	    return _env("PG_DICED_VM_REPO", "/workspace/POLARIS")
124	
125	
126	def breadth_vm_command() -> str:
127	    """PROBE 1 -- the exact VM command for the front-half breadth proof.
128	
129	    No --stop-after flag exists, so this runs ONE non-simple query end-to-end (cheap vs the
130	    $40 8-query sweep). PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE=1 (default ON) so retrieval.relevance_gate
131	    populates; PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP / PG_LIVE_FETCH_CAP are LEFT UNSET so the fetch budget
132	    defaults to the new 200 (the breadth lever); PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR left unset (default 0.30).
133	    """
134	    slug = _env("PG_PROBE_BREADTH_SLUG", "drb_72_ai_labor")
135	    out_root = _env("PG_PROBE_BREADTH_OUT", "outputs/probe_breadth")
136	    return (
137	        f"{_ssh_prefix()} 'cd {_remote_repo()} && "
138	        f"env -u OPENAI_API_KEY "
139	        f"PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE=1 "
140	        f"python scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py "
141	        f"--only {slug} --out-root {out_root} "
142	        f"2>&1 | tee /tmp/probe_breadth.log'"
143	    )
144	
145	
146	def kimi_vm_command() -> str:
147	    """PROBE 2 -- the exact VM command for the kimi 4-role D8 seam smoke.
148	
149	    LAUNCH VIA run_gate_b.py, NOT run_honest_sweep_r3.py --pathB-gate. run_gate_b.py is the ONLY
150	    slate-applying entrypoint: its main() + run_gate_b_query CONSTRUCT the kimi judge transport (via
151	    benchmark_verifier_lineup), call enable_four_role_mode() (PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE=1 -> the seam
152	    activates), AND apply_full_capability_benchmark_slate() (so PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_ENABLED is ON
153	    -> the kimi judge adjudicates the FULL breadth basket, not a narrow report). run_honest_sweep_r3.py
154	    --pathB-gate wraps run_one_query in pathB_runner.gate_around_question, which does NOT apply the
155	    slate -> enrichment OFF -> the cite-breadth + kimi-judge path is not really exercised. run_gate_b.py
156	    has its OWN --resume (-> run_gate_b_query(resume=...) -> run_one_query(resume=...)), so it re-enters
157	    from the SMALL banked corpus_snapshot under {out_root}/<domain>/{slug}/ with NO re-fetch (cheap).
158	    PG_BENCHMARK_JUDGE_MODEL is LEFT UNSET so the default moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 judge is used. The seam
159	    wall is raised generously for the big/slow judge (PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, LAW VI override).
160	    """
161	    slug = _env("PG_PROBE_KIMI_SLUG", "drb_72_ai_labor")
162	    out_root = _env("PG_PROBE_KIMI_OUT", "outputs/probe_kimi_resume")
163	    seam_wall = _env_int("PG_PROBE_SEAM_DEADLINE_S", 3600)
164	    return (
165	        f"{_ssh_prefix()} 'cd {_remote_repo()} && "
166	        f"env -u OPENAI_API_KEY "
167	        f"PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS={seam_wall} "
168	        f"python scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py "
169	        f"--only {slug} --resume --out-root {out_root} "
170	        f"2>&1 | tee /tmp/probe_kimi.log'"
171	    )
172	
173	
174	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
175	# PURE assertion functions (testable: take loaded dicts/lists, return list[Check]).
176	# These read the NEW manifest fields (retrieval.relevance_gate from A5c; the seam telemetry
177	# sidecars from A6). No I/O, no spend.
178	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
179	
180	def _active_fetch_cap(caps: dict) -> int:
181	    """The active PG_LIVE_FETCH_CAP value disclosed in retrieval_caps.search_truncations (the
182	    fetch BUDGET). 0 if absent -> the budget check then requires dropped_pre_fetch == 0."""
183	    for t in caps.get("search_truncations", []) or []:
184	        if isinstance(t, dict) and t.get("cap") == "PG_LIVE_FETCH_CAP":
185	            try:
186	                return int(t.get("value") or 0)
187	            except (TypeError, ValueError):
188	                return 0
189	    return 0
190	
191	
192	def breadth_manifest_checks(manifest: dict, *, legacy_fetch_floor: int = 40) -> list[Check]:
193	    """The §-1.3 BREADTH proof, read from a FRESH finalized manifest.json.
194	
195	    1. retrieval.relevance_gate is PRESENT (A5c persisted it to the DURABLE manifest -- a
196	       pre-A5c manifest or a B4-OFF run has it None).
197	    2. relevance_gate.demoted_fetched_to_fill > 0  == below-floor sources reached the fetch
198	       BUDGET and were FETCHED -> the §-1.3 demote-not-drop FIRED.
199	    3. retrieval_caps.dropped_pre_fetch is fully attributable to the DISCLOSED fetch budget,
200	       NOT a hard floor-drop: dropped_pre_fetch <= max(0, discovered - fetch_cap - failed).
201	       (When discovered <= budget this requires dropped_pre_fetch == 0.)
202	    4. candidates_fetched is materially > the legacy 40 (the raised budget actually fetched
203	       more than the old cap).
204	    """
205	    retrieval = manifest.get("retrieval") or {}
206	    caps = retrieval.get("retrieval_caps") or {}
207	    rg = retrieval.get("relevance_gate")
208	    present = isinstance(rg, dict)
209	    checks: list[Check] = []
210	
211	    checks.append(Check(
212	        "breadth.relevance_gate_present",
213	        present,
214	        ("retrieval.relevance_gate is PRESENT (A5c durable-manifest persistence)"
215	         if present else
216	         "retrieval.relevance_gate is MISSING/None -- B4 gate OFF, only-seeds, or a "
217	         "pre-A5c manifest; the §-1.3 demote proof cannot be read"),
218	    ))
219	
220	    demoted = int((rg or {}).get("demoted_fetched_to_fill", 0) or 0) if present else 0
221	    checks.append(Check(
222	        "breadth.demote_fired",
223	        present and demoted > 0,
224	        (f"relevance_gate.demoted_fetched_to_fill={demoted} "
225	         f"(>0 == below-floor candidates reached the fetch BUDGET and were FETCHED -> the "
226	         f"§-1.3 demote-not-drop fired; ==0 means the floor was still a pre-fetch cut OR the "
227	         f"corpus had no below-floor sources)"),
228	    ))
229	
230	    discovered = int(caps.get("candidates_discovered", 0) or 0)
231	    fetched = int(caps.get("fetched", retrieval.get("fetched", 0)) or 0)
232	    failed = int(caps.get("failed", retrieval.get("failed", 0)) or 0)
233	    dropped = int(caps.get("dropped_pre_fetch", 0) or 0)
234	    fetch_cap = _active_fetch_cap(caps)
235	    budget_overflow = max(0, discovered - fetch_cap - failed) if fetch_cap > 0 else 0
236	    ok_budget = dropped <= budget_overflow
237	    checks.append(Check(
238	        "breadth.dropped_is_budget_not_floor",
239	        ok_budget,
240	        (f"dropped_pre_fetch={dropped} <= budget_overflow={budget_overflow} "
241	         f"(candidates_discovered={discovered} fetch_cap={fetch_cap} failed={failed}); "
242	         f"a drop BEYOND the disclosed budget would be a §-1.3-banned hard floor-FILTER"),
243	    ))
244	
245	    checks.append(Check(
246	        "breadth.fetched_above_legacy_40",
247	        fetched > legacy_fetch_floor,
248	        (f"candidates_fetched={fetched} > legacy_floor={legacy_fetch_floor} "
249	         f"(the raised budget fetched more than the old 40-cap)"),
250	    ))
251	
252	    return checks
253	
254	
255	def kimi_seam_checks(
256	    manifest: dict,
257	    rate_limit_telemetry: Optional[dict],
258	    role_calls: list[dict],
259	    *,
260	    max_429: int = 0,
261	) -> list[Check]:
262	    """The KIMI 4-role D8 seam proof, read from a FRESH --resume run_dir.
263	
264	    1. status != abort_role_transport_exhausted  (the seam did not die).
265	    2. four_role_seam_inert is not True           (a transport WAS injected -- run_gate_b.py).
266	    3. no held_reason starts with "seam_"         (proxy for the runtime _seam_held_reason is None).
267	    4. no four_role_seam_unadjudicated disclosed gap (the judge bound for the claims).
268	    5. rate_limit_hits_total <= max_429            (no 429-storm; the kimi 21-provider point).
269	    6. four_role_evaluation.final_verdicts non-empty.
270	    7. every judge-role call returned a parseable verdict body (non-empty raw_text -- no 400 on
271	       the bare reasoning block). The distinct served_model count is reported as the (best-effort)
272	       multi-provider proxy; the BINDING check is parseability ("OR at minimum ...").
273	    """
274	    fr = manifest.get("four_role_evaluation") or {}
275	    status = manifest.get("status", "")
276	    checks: list[Check] = []
277	
278	    checks.append(Check(
279	        "kimi.not_transport_exhausted",
280	        status != _TRANSPORT_EXHAUSTED,
281	        f"manifest.status={status!r} (must NOT be {_TRANSPORT_EXHAUSTED})",
282	    ))
283	
284	    inert = bool(manifest.get("four_role_seam_inert", False))
285	    checks.append(Check(
286	        "kimi.seam_not_inert",
287	        not inert,
288	        (f"four_role_seam_inert={inert} (False == a real RoleTransport was injected by "
289	         f"Gate-B; True == PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE on but no transport -> seam never ran)"),
290	    ))
291	
292	    held = fr.get("held_reasons") or []
293	    seam_held = [h for h in held if isinstance(h, str) and h.startswith("seam_")]
294	    checks.append(Check(
295	        "kimi.no_seam_held_reason",
296	        not seam_held,
297	        (f"four_role_evaluation.held_reasons seam_*={seam_held or '[]'} "
298	         f"(a seam_timeout / seam_error:* held_reason == the seam tore)"),
299	    ))
300	
301	    gaps = list(manifest.get("disclosed_gaps") or [])
302	    gaps += [g for g in (fr.get("gaps") or []) if isinstance(g, str)]
303	    seam_gap = [g for g in gaps if isinstance(g, str) and _SEAM_UNADJUDICATED in g]
304	    checks.append(Check(
305	        "kimi.no_seam_unadjudicated_gap",
306	        not seam_gap,
307	        (f"{_SEAM_UNADJUDICATED} disclosed gaps={len(seam_gap)} "
308	         f"(0 == the judge adjudicated; >0 == the seam could not bind the judge)"),
309	    ))
310	
311	    total_429 = int((rate_limit_telemetry or {}).get("rate_limit_hits_total", -1))
312	    have_telemetry = rate_limit_telemetry is not None
313	    checks.append(Check(
314	        "kimi.no_429_storm",
315	        have_telemetry and 0 <= total_429 <= max_429,
316	        (f"four_role_rate_limit_telemetry.rate_limit_hits_total={total_429} <= max={max_429} "
317	         f"(telemetry_present={have_telemetry}; a missing sidecar == the seam never completed)"),
318	    ))
319	
320	    fv = fr.get("final_verdicts") or {}
321	    checks.append(Check(
322	        "kimi.final_verdicts_nonempty",
323	        len(fv) > 0,
324	        f"four_role_evaluation.final_verdicts count={len(fv)} (>0 == the judge settled claims)",
325	    ))
326	
327	    judge_calls = [c for c in role_calls if isinstance(c, dict) and c.get("role") == "judge"]
328	    used_fallback = False
329	    if not judge_calls:
330	        # robustness: if the role label differs, fall back to ALL role calls
331	        judge_calls = [c for c in role_calls if isinstance(c, dict)]
332	        used_fallback = True
333	    parseable = [c for c in judge_calls if (c.get("raw_text") or "").strip()]
334	    served = sorted({c.get("served_model") for c in judge_calls if c.get("served_model")})
335	    all_parseable = len(judge_calls) > 0 and len(parseable) == len(judge_calls)
336	    checks.append(Check(
337	        "kimi.judge_calls_parseable",
338	        all_parseable,
339	        (f"judge_calls={len(judge_calls)}{' (role==judge absent; used ALL role calls)' if used_fallback else ''} "
340	         f"parseable(non-empty raw_text)={len(parseable)}; distinct served_model="
341	         f"{served} (>1 == provider load-balancing; multi-provider is a proxy, parseability "
342	         f"is the binding check)"),
343	    ))
344	
345	    return checks
346	
347	
348	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
349	# I/O wrappers + diced-preflight shell-out (offline, read-only, no spend)
350	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
351	
352	def _resolve_run_dir(d: Path) -> Path:
353	    """The run_dir holds manifest.json directly. If the operator passes the --out-root instead,
354	    descend to the single child holding manifest.json. Fail loud on ambiguity/absence."""
355	    if (d / "manifest.json").is_file():
356	        return d
357	    children = [c for c in sorted(d.iterdir()) if c.is_dir() and (c / "manifest.json").is_file()] if d.is_dir() else []
358	    if len(children) == 1:
359	        return children[0]
360	    if not children:
361	        raise FileNotFoundError(
362	            f"no manifest.json in {d} nor in any immediate child -- pass the per-query run_dir"
363	        )
364	    raise FileNotFoundError(
365	        f"ambiguous: {len(children)} child run_dirs hold a manifest.json under {d}; "
366	        f"pass the specific per-query run_dir ({', '.join(c.name for c in children)})"
367	    )
368	
369	
370	def _load_json(path: Path) -> Any:
371	    return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
372	
373	
374	def _load_jsonl(path: Path) -> list[dict]:
375	    if not path.is_file():
376	        return []
377	    out: list[dict] = []
378	    for line in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
379	        line = line.strip()
380	        if not line:
381	            continue
382	        try:
383	            out.append(json.loads(line))
384	        except json.JSONDecodeError:
385	            continue
386	    return out
387	
388	
389	def diced_preflight_checks(run_dir: Path, diced_path: Path, dice_names: list[str]) -> list[Check]:
390	    """Shell out to the (offline, read-only) diced preflight on the FRESH run_dir and assert the
391	    NAMED dice are GREEN. The diced preflight NEVER makes a paid call (its own docstring); we gate
392	    ONLY on the ``dice_names`` the caller passes (other dice may be RED for unrelated reasons on a
393	    single-query dir; each probe asserts only the dice its LAUNCH PATH can actually flip GREEN).
394	
395	    WHICH DICE PER PROBE (the diced-leg/launch-path coupling -- Codex P1 #1344 wave-3):
396	      * BREADTH probe -> ``["D3_relevance_gate_fetch_budget"]`` ONLY. The breadth probe launches the
397	        NO-slate ``run_honest_sweep_r3.py`` (PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_ENABLED stays OFF), so its proof
398	        is the FRONT-HALF demote-not-drop + fetch-budget dice (D3). The BACK-HALF cite-breadth dice
399	        ``D2_composition_breadth`` reads report.md cited/pool ratio and is GREEN only once the slate's
400	        post-gen weighted-enrichment fires -> it would stay RED here even on a correctly-fixed
401	        pipeline (a false-FAIL), so it is NOT asserted on the breadth probe.
402	      * KIMI probe -> ``["D2_composition_breadth"]``. The kimi probe launches via ``run_gate_b.py``
403	        WITH the slate -> enrichment ON -> the composer surfaces the weighted tail, so the cite-breadth
404	        dice can legitimately go GREEN there.
405	
406	    A concurrent workflow owns pipeline_diced_preflight.py -- we only CONSUME its verdict."""
407	    if not diced_path.is_file():
408	        return [Check("diced.preflight_present", False, f"diced preflight not found at {diced_path}")]
409	    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="probe_diced_") as td:
410	        out = Path(td) / "diced.json"
411	        cmd = [sys.executable, str(diced_path), "--fixture", str(run_dir), "--json", str(out)]
412	        try:
413	            proc = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=900)
414	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
415	            return [Check("diced.ran", False, "diced preflight timed out (>900s)")]
416	        if not out.is_file():
417	            tail = (proc.stderr or proc.stdout or "")[-400:]
418	            return [Check("diced.ran", False,
419	                          f"diced preflight produced no --json (exit={proc.returncode}): {tail}")]
420	        payload = _load_json(out)
421	    offline = {r.get("name"): r.get("status") for r in payload.get("offline", []) or []}
422	    checks: list[Check] = []
423	    for dice in dice_names:
424	        st = offline.get(dice, "ABSENT")
425	        checks.append(Check(
426	            f"diced.{dice}", st == GREEN,
427	            f"{dice}={st} (was RED on the old banked fixture; GREEN proves the fix landed)",
428	        ))
429	    return checks
430	
431	
432	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
433	# PRINT mode
434	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
435	
436	def _print_banner() -> None:
437	    print("=" * 92)
438	    print("  SMALL-SCALE VALIDATION PROBE (I-deepfix-001 #1344) -- NO SPEND, BUILD + OFFLINE-ASSERT")
439	    print("  This runner launches NO run, loads NO model, calls NO OpenRouter endpoint.")
440	    print("  The VM runs below are the OPERATOR's to launch (heavy runs are VM-only, §8.4).")
441	    print("=" * 92)
442	
443	
444	def print_breadth(_argv_note: str = "") -> None:
445	    print("\n--- PROBE 1: BREADTH front-half (the §-1.3 demote + raised-budget proof, cheap) ---")
446	    print("WHY one query end-to-end: run_honest_sweep_r3.py has NO --stop-after flag and the")
447	    print("corpus_snapshot checkpoint does NOT carry retrieval.relevance_gate; the §-1.3 demote")
448	    print("proof lives ONLY in the finalized manifest.json. A single NON-simple query is the cheap")
449	    print("unit (far below the $40 8-query sweep). Do NOT pick a trivially-simple slug: the sweep")
450	    print("simple-router would drop the fetch budget to PG_SIMPLE_FETCH_CAP=40.")
451	    print("NOTE on the launcher: this FRONT-HALF breadth proof is FINE via run_honest_sweep_r3.py.")
452	    print("The §-1.3 demote + fetch-budget mechanism lives in RETRIEVAL (manifest.retrieval.*), not")
453	    print("in the post-gen breadth ENRICHMENT, so it fires with PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE=1 set")
454	    print("explicitly + PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP defaulting to 200 -- the slate (which run_honest_sweep_r3.py")
455	    print("does NOT apply -> PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_ENABLED stays OFF) is not needed for THIS proof.")
456	    print("(The kimi seam probe below DOES need the slate, so it launches via run_gate_b.py.)")
457	    print("\nVM COMMAND:")
458	    print("  " + breadth_vm_command())
459	    print("\nTHEN assert offline on the fresh run_dir (this runner, no spend):")
460	    print("  python scripts/small_scale_validation_probe.py --probe breadth --assert-dir "
461	          "<fresh_run_dir>")
462	    print("\nASSERTIONS (fail-loud):")
463	    print("  - manifest.retrieval.relevance_gate is PRESENT")
464	    print("  - relevance_gate.demoted_fetched_to_fill > 0          (the §-1.3 demote fired)")
465	    print("  - retrieval_caps.dropped_pre_fetch <= the disclosed fetch budget (no hard floor-drop)")
466	    print("  - candidates_fetched materially > the legacy 40")
467	    print("  - diced preflight on the fresh dir: D3_relevance_gate_fetch_budget == GREEN")
468	    print("    (the FRONT-HALF demote+budget dice; the BACK-HALF cite-breadth dice "
469	          "D2_composition_breadth belongs to the KIMI probe, which runs WITH the slate)")
470	
471	
472	def print_kimi(_argv_note: str = "") -> None:
473	    print("\n--- PROBE 2: KIMI 4-role D8 seam smoke (cheap live proof the seam completes) ---")
474	    print("WHY run_gate_b.py --resume (NOT run_honest_sweep_r3.py --pathB-gate): run_gate_b.py is the")
475	    print("ONLY slate-applying entrypoint. Its main()/run_gate_b_query construct the kimi judge")
476	    print("transport (benchmark_verifier_lineup), call enable_four_role_mode() (PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE=1),")
477	    print("AND apply_full_capability_benchmark_slate() (PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_ENABLED ON -> the judge")
478	    print("adjudicates the FULL breadth basket). run_honest_sweep_r3.py --pathB-gate does NOT apply the")
479	    print("slate -> enrichment OFF -> the kimi-judge path is not really exercised. run_gate_b.py has its")
480	    print("OWN --resume, so it re-enters from a SMALL banked corpus_snapshot (NO re-fetch, cheap).")
481	    print("PG_BENCHMARK_JUDGE_MODEL is left UNSET so the default moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 judge is used.")
482	    print("This is the LIVE companion to the diced preflight's D4_four_role_judge_seam_LIVE dice.")
483	    print("\nVM COMMAND (point --out-root at a dir already holding <domain>/<slug>/corpus_snapshot.json):")
484	    print("  " + kimi_vm_command())
485	    print("\nTHEN assert offline on the produced run_dir (this runner, no spend):")
486	    print("  python scripts/small_scale_validation_probe.py --probe kimi --assert-dir "
487	          "<resume_run_dir>")
488	    print("\nASSERTIONS (fail-loud):")
489	    print("  - manifest.status != abort_role_transport_exhausted")
490	    print("  - four_role_seam_inert is not True; no seam_* held_reason; no "
491	          "four_role_seam_unadjudicated gap")
492	    print("  - four_role_rate_limit_telemetry.rate_limit_hits_total == 0   (no 429-storm)")
493	    print("  - four_role_evaluation.final_verdicts non-empty")
494	    print("  - every judge call returned a parseable verdict body (no 400 on the bare reasoning "
495	          "block); distinct served_model reported as the multi-provider proxy")
496	    print("  - diced preflight on the produced dir: D2_composition_breadth == GREEN")
497	    print("    (the slate is ON here -> post-gen weighted-enrichment fires -> the composer surfaces "
498	          "the cite-breadth tail; this dice cannot go GREEN on the no-slate breadth probe)")
499	
500	
501	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
502	# ASSERT mode
503	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
504	
505	def assert_breadth(run_dir: Path, diced_path: Path) -> list[Check]:
506	    manifest = _load_json(run_dir / "manifest.json")
507	    legacy_floor = _env_int("PG_PROBE_LEGACY_FETCH_FLOOR", 40)
508	    checks = breadth_manifest_checks(manifest, legacy_fetch_floor=legacy_floor)
509	    # FRONT-HALF diced leg ONLY: the breadth probe launches the NO-slate run_honest_sweep_r3.py, so
510	    # the BACK-HALF cite-breadth dice (D2_composition_breadth) cannot go GREEN here (enrichment OFF)
511	    # -> asserting it would be a false-FAIL. Assert the FRONT-HALF demote+budget dice only; the
512	    # cite-breadth dice is asserted by the KIMI probe (slate ON -> enrichment surfaces the tail).
513	    checks += diced_preflight_checks(run_dir, diced_path, ["D3_relevance_gate_fetch_budget"])
514	    return checks
515	
516	
517	def assert_kimi(run_dir: Path, diced_path: Path) -> list[Check]:
518	    manifest = _load_json(run_dir / "manifest.json")
519	    rl_path = run_dir / "four_role_rate_limit_telemetry.json"
520	    rate_limit = _load_json(rl_path) if rl_path.is_file() else None
521	    role_calls = _load_jsonl(run_dir / "four_role_role_calls.jsonl")
522	    max_429 = _env_int("PG_PROBE_MAX_429", 0)
523	    checks = kimi_seam_checks(manifest, rate_limit, role_calls, max_429=max_429)
524	    # BACK-HALF cite-breadth diced leg: the kimi probe launches via run_gate_b.py WITH the slate ->
525	    # PG_BREADTH_ENRICHMENT_ENABLED ON -> the composer surfaces the weighted tail, so the post-gen
526	    # D2_composition_breadth dice can legitimately go GREEN here (it cannot on the no-slate breadth probe).
527	    checks += diced_preflight_checks(run_dir, diced_path, ["D2_composition_breadth"])
528	    return checks
529	
530	
531	def _print_checks(title: str, checks: list[Check]) -> bool:
532	    name_w = max([len(c.name) for c in checks] + [24])
533	    print(f"\n  {title}")
534	    print("  " + "-" * (name_w + 8))
535	    for c in checks:
536	        mark = "GREEN" if c.passed else "RED  "
537	        print(f"  {mark}  {c.name:<{name_w}}  {c.detail}")
538	    return all(c.passed for c in checks)
539	
540	
541	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
542	# Runner
543	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
544	
545	def main(argv: Optional[list[str]] = None) -> int:
546	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(
547	        description="Small-scale validation probe for the breadth (A5) + kimi-judge (A6) fixes. "
548	                    "PRINTS the VM command(s); with --assert-dir runs OFFLINE assertions. NEVER spends.",
549	    )
550	    ap.add_argument("--probe", choices=["breadth", "kimi", "both"], required=True,
551	                    help="which fix to probe.")
552	    ap.add_argument("--assert-dir", default=None,
553	                    help="run OFFLINE assertions on this ALREADY-PRODUCED run_dir (or its out-root). "
554	                         "Omit to PRINT the VM command(s) instead.")
555	    ap.add_argument("--diced-preflight", default=str(_DEFAULT_DICED_PREFLIGHT),
556	                    help="path to pipeline_diced_preflight.py (breadth assert shells out to it).")
557	    ap.add_argument("--json", default=None, help="write a machine-readable result sidecar here.")
558	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
559	
560	    probes = ["breadth", "kimi"] if args.probe == "both" else [args.probe]
561	
562	    # ---- PRINT mode -----------------------------------------------------------------------
563	    if not args.assert_dir:
564	        _print_banner()
565	        if "breadth" in probes:
566	            print_breadth()
567	        if "kimi" in probes:
568	            print_kimi()
569	        print("\n" + "=" * 92)
570	        print("  PRINT mode only -- nothing was executed, no spend. Launch the VM command(s) above,")
571	        print("  then re-run with --assert-dir <fresh_run_dir> to validate offline.")
572	        print("=" * 92)
573	        return 0
574	
575	    # ---- ASSERT mode (offline, read-only) -------------------------------------------------
576	    raw_dir = Path(args.assert_dir)
577	    if not raw_dir.exists():
578	        print(f"[probe] HARNESS ERROR: --assert-dir not found: {raw_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
579	        return 2
580	    try:
581	        run_dir = _resolve_run_dir(raw_dir)
582	    except FileNotFoundError as exc:
583	        print(f"[probe] HARNESS ERROR: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
584	        return 2
585	
586	    diced_path = Path(args.diced_preflight)
587	    print(f"[probe] assert mode on run_dir: {run_dir}")
588	    all_results: dict[str, list[Check]] = {}
589	    overall_ok = True
590	
591	    if "breadth" in probes:
592	        try:
593	            checks = assert_breadth(run_dir, diced_path)
594	        except Exception as exc:  # fail-loud: an un-runnable assertion is RED, never skipped
595	            checks = [Check("breadth.harness", False, f"EXCEPTION {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")]
596	        all_results["breadth"] = checks
597	        overall_ok &= _print_checks("PROBE 1 -- BREADTH (front-half §-1.3 demote + budget)", checks)
598	
599	    if "kimi" in probes:
600	        try:
601	            checks = assert_kimi(run_dir, diced_path)
602	        except Exception as exc:
603	            checks = [Check("kimi.harness", False, f"EXCEPTION {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")]
604	        all_results["kimi"] = checks
605	        overall_ok &= _print_checks("PROBE 2 -- KIMI (4-role D8 seam completion)", checks)
606	
607	    verdict = "PASS" if overall_ok else "FAIL"
608	    n_red = sum(1 for cs in all_results.values() for c in cs if not c.passed)
609	    print(f"\n  PROBE RESULT: {verdict}  ({n_red} RED check(s))")
610	    print("=" * 92)
611	
612	    if args.json:
613	        payload = {
614	            "verdict": verdict,
615	            "run_dir": str(run_dir),
616	            "probes": {
617	                name: [c.__dict__ for c in cs] for name, cs in all_results.items()
618	            },
619	        }
620	        Path(args.json).write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
621	        print(f"[probe] wrote sidecar -> {args.json}")
622	
623	    # Fail-loud: exit non-zero on any RED check.
624	    return 0 if overall_ok else 1
625	
626	
627	if __name__ == "__main__":
628	    sys.exit(main())
629

1	"""Operator-launched smoke for PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE=log_only calibration.
2	
3	Closes Codex PR #906 iter-5 follow-up #1: "Real-run smoke with
4	PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE=log_only to calibrate refusal rates."
5	
6	Why operator-launched (not autonomous):
7	    Real V4 Pro API calls cost actual $$. Per CLAUDE.md §8.4 + the
8	    operator's stated preference ("control the resources carefully"),
9	    autonomous heavy ML/API runs are forbidden. This script is the
10	    canonical entry point the operator invokes when ready.
11	
12	What this captures:
13	    1. PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE=log_only — validator populates SectionResult
14	       atom_validation_result + writes gaps.json sidecar; report.md is
15	       UNCHANGED from pre-Step-3b behavior. Safe to compare reports
16	       side-by-side with off-mode baseline.
17	    2. Single-vector smoke (Tirzepatide T2DM by default — known to have
18	       atomizable claims from SURPASS-2 evidence).
19	    3. Aggregates per-section refusal_count + soft_mismatch_count +
20	       allowed_count into a calibration summary printed at end.
21	
22	Usage (manual operator invocation):
23	    PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE=log_only python scripts/smoke_atom_refusal_log_only.py
24	    # OR with explicit vector:
25	    PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE=log_only python scripts/smoke_atom_refusal_log_only.py \\
26	        --vector clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm
27	
28	Output:
29	    - outputs/honest_sweep_r3/clinical/<vector>/gaps.json
30	        (per Codex APPROVE_DESIGN schema)
31	    - stdout: refusal-rate calibration summary
32	
33	Decision criteria for flipping to PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE=strict (per
34	Codex Step 3b iter-1 advice):
35	    - Average refusal_rate < 30% across sections (else V4 Pro's atom
36	      compliance needs prompt tightening before strict)
37	    - Zero "missing_atom_citation" reasons where a valid atom_NNN
38	      should have existed (would indicate validator false-positive)
39	    - SOFT mismatch count low (else the SOFT layer's value-token
40	      matching is too strict)
41	"""
42	
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import argparse
46	import json
47	import os
48	import subprocess
49	import sys
50	from pathlib import Path
51	
52	
53	def main() -> int:
54	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(
55	        description=(
56	            "Operator smoke for atom-refusal log_only calibration. "
57	            "Real V4 Pro API calls — operator-launched only."
58	        ),
59	    )
60	    p.add_argument(
61	        "--vector",
62	        default="clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm",
63	        help="Vector slug to run (default: clinical_tirzepatide_t2dm)",
64	    )
65	    p.add_argument(
66	        "--out-root",
67	        default="outputs/honest_sweep_r3",
68	        help="Output root (default: outputs/honest_sweep_r3) — matches "
69	             "run_honest_sweep_r3.py's flag exactly.",
70	    )
71	    args = p.parse_args()
72	
73	    # Operator must set the mode explicitly — script does not flip the
74	    # flag for them. Defensive check.
75	    mode = os.environ.get("PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE", "off").strip().lower()
76	    if mode != "log_only":
77	        print(
78	            "ERROR: PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE must be 'log_only' for this smoke. "
79	            f"Got {mode!r}. Set via:\n"
80	            "  PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE=log_only python scripts/smoke_atom_refusal_log_only.py",
81	            file=sys.stderr,
82	        )
83	        return 2
84	
85	    # Sanity check the canonical sweep entry exists
86	    sweep_script = Path("scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py")
87	    if not sweep_script.exists():
88	        print(f"ERROR: {sweep_script} not found — wrong CWD?", file=sys.stderr)
89	        return 2
90	
91	    # Launch the single-vector sweep
92	    cmd = [
93	        sys.executable,
94	        str(sweep_script),
95	        "--only",
96	        args.vector,
97	        "--out-root",
98	        args.out_root,
99	    ]
100	    print(f"[smoke] launching: {' '.join(cmd)}", flush=True)
101	    print(f"[smoke] PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE={mode}", flush=True)
102	    rc = subprocess.run(cmd, env=os.environ.copy()).returncode
103	    if rc != 0:
104	        print(f"[smoke] sweep returned non-zero exit: {rc}", file=sys.stderr)
105	        return rc
106	
107	    # Locate the produced gaps.json
108	    candidate_dirs = list(Path(args.out_root).rglob(args.vector))
109	    if not candidate_dirs:
110	        print(
111	            f"[smoke] WARN: no run directory matching {args.vector!r} found "
112	            f"under {args.out_root}",
113	            file=sys.stderr,
114	        )
115	        return 0
116	    run_dir = candidate_dirs[0]
117	    gaps_path = run_dir / "gaps.json"
118	    if not gaps_path.exists():
119	        print(
120	            f"[smoke] WARN: gaps.json not produced at {gaps_path}. "
121	            f"Atom validator may have skipped (empty catalog?) or hooked "
122	            f"path didn't trigger. Check run_log.txt for "
123	            f"'[gaps]' or '[multi_section] I-gen-005 Step 3b atom validation' lines.",
124	            file=sys.stderr,
125	        )
126	        return 0
127	
128	    # Print calibration summary
129	    with gaps_path.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
130	        gaps = json.load(f)
131	
132	    print("")
133	    print("=" * 70)
134	    print("ATOM-REFUSAL LOG_ONLY CALIBRATION SUMMARY")
135	    print("=" * 70)
136	    print(f"document_id:    {gaps.get('document_id')}")
137	    print(f"generated_at:   {gaps.get('generated_at')}")
138	    totals = gaps.get("totals", {})
139	    print(f"total_sentences: {totals.get('total_sentences', 0)}")
140	    print(f"  refused:       {totals.get('refused', 0)}")
141	    print(f"  soft_mismatch: {totals.get('soft_mismatch', 0)}")
142	    print(f"  allowed:       {totals.get('allowed', 0)}")
143	
144	    total_s = totals.get("total_sentences", 0)
145	    if total_s > 0:
146	        refusal_rate = totals.get("refused", 0) / total_s
147	        print(f"  refusal_rate:  {refusal_rate:.1%}")
148	        # Codex iter-1 advice threshold
149	        if refusal_rate < 0.30:
150	            print("  ->strict-rollout candidate (refusal_rate < 30%)")
151	        else:
152	            print(
153	                "  ->V4 Pro atom compliance needs work BEFORE flipping to "
154	                "strict (refusal_rate >= 30%)"
155	            )
156	
157	    print("")
158	    print("Per-section breakdown:")
159	    for sec in gaps.get("sections", []):
160	        s = sec.get("summary", {})
161	        title = sec.get("section_title", "?")
162	        print(
163	            f"  {title!r}: "
164	            f"refused={s.get('refused', 0)} "
165	            f"soft={s.get('soft_mismatch', 0)} "
166	            f"allowed={s.get('allowed', 0)}"
167	        )
168	
169	    # Top refusal reasons (a sample)
170	    reason_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
171	    for sec in gaps.get("sections", []):
172	        for claim in sec.get("claims", []):
173	            r = claim.get("reason")
174	            if r and r != "no_violation":
175	                reason_counts[r] = reason_counts.get(r, 0) + 1
176	
177	    if reason_counts:
178	        print("")
179	        print("Top non-violation reasons:")
180	        for reason, n in sorted(reason_counts.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
181	            print(f"  {reason}: {n}")
182	
183	    print("=" * 70)
184	    print(f"Full gaps.json: {gaps_path}")
185	    print("")
186	    print(
187	        "Next: if refusal_rate looks reasonable, flip to "
188	        "PG_ATOM_REFUSAL_MODE=strict and rerun the same sweep. Refused "
189	        "sentences will be replaced with refusal disclosure blocks in "
190	        "report.md."
191	    )
192	
193	    return 0
194	
195	
196	if __name__ == "__main__":
197	    raise SystemExit(main())
198

1	"""Strip changelog markers from a diff or source file before sending
2	to Codex for review (autoloop V3 — Procedural Independence Protocol).
3	
4	Per `.codex/REVIEW_BRIEF_FORMAT.md` + sister-project research:
5	prior-round changelog markers (`// CORRECTED v2 per Codex round-1`,
6	`# Codex round-3 LOW fix`, etc.) anchor the reviewer toward
7	"already addressed" framings even when the underlying patch is
8	incomplete. Procedural Independence: remove them mechanically
9	rather than asking Codex to mentally suppress.
10	
11	Usage:
12	    python scripts/strip_changelog_markers.py path/to/diff.txt
13	    git diff main | python scripts/strip_changelog_markers.py -
14	
15	Patterns stripped (lines matching ANY of these are removed entirely
16	when the line is a comment line, OR the marker text is removed
17	when embedded in a multi-marker line):
18	
19	    Codex round-N <verdict>           e.g. "Codex round-2 HIGH fix"
20	    CORRECTED v\\d+                    e.g. "CORRECTED v3 per Codex"
21	    round-N (LOW|MED|HIGH|MEDIUM)     e.g. "round-1 MED"
22	    R\\d+ <verdict>                    e.g. "R2 HIGH"
23	    iter-N findings                   e.g. "iter-12 finding"
24	    v\\d+ closes round-N <verdict>     e.g. "v3 closes round-2 LOW"
25	
26	Lines whose entire content (after comment markers) matches the
27	pattern are dropped. Inline embeddings have just the marker
28	substring stripped, leaving the rest of the line intact.
29	"""
30	
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import argparse
34	import re
35	import sys
36	from pathlib import Path
37	
38	# Order matters — most specific first.
39	_MARKER_PATTERNS: list[re.Pattern[str]] = [
40	    re.compile(
41	        r"\bv\d+\s+(?:closes|close|closing)\s+round-?\d+\s+"
42	        r"(?:LOW|MED|MEDIUM|HIGH|BLOCKED)\b",
43	        re.IGNORECASE,
44	    ),
45	    re.compile(
46	        r"\bCodex\s+round-?\d+\s+(?:LOW|MED|MEDIUM|HIGH|BLOCKED|fix)?",
47	        re.IGNORECASE,
48	    ),
49	    re.compile(
50	        r"\bCORRECTED\s+v\d+(?:\s+per\s+Codex)?",
51	        re.IGNORECASE,
52	    ),
53	    re.compile(
54	        r"\b(?:R|round-?)\d+\s+(?:LOW|MED|MEDIUM|HIGH|BLOCKED)\s+(?:fix|finding)?",
55	        re.IGNORECASE,
56	    ),
57	    re.compile(
58	        r"\biter-?\d+\s+(?:finding|findings|fix|fixes)",
59	        re.IGNORECASE,
60	    ),
61	]
62	
63	_COMMENT_LINE = re.compile(
64	    r"^(\s*(?:#|//|--|/\*|\*)\s*)(.*?)(\s*\*/?\s*)?$"
65	)
66	
67	
68	def _strip_line(line: str) -> str | None:
69	    """Returns:
70	      - None if the entire line should be dropped (comment-only +
71	        full content matches a marker)
72	      - the cleaned line otherwise (with any embedded markers
73	        stripped)
74	    """
75	    m = _COMMENT_LINE.match(line)
76	    if m:
77	        prefix, body, suffix = m.group(1), m.group(2), m.group(3) or ""
78	        cleaned_body = body
79	        for pat in _MARKER_PATTERNS:
80	            cleaned_body = pat.sub("", cleaned_body)
81	        cleaned_body = re.sub(r"\s{2,}", " ", cleaned_body).strip()
82	        if not cleaned_body:
83	            return None
84	        return f"{prefix}{cleaned_body}{suffix}\n"
85	
86	    cleaned = line
87	    for pat in _MARKER_PATTERNS:
88	        cleaned = pat.sub("", cleaned)
89	    cleaned = re.sub(r"  +", " ", cleaned)
90	    return cleaned
91	
92	
93	def strip_text(text: str) -> str:
94	    out_lines: list[str] = []
95	    for raw in text.splitlines(keepends=True):
96	        cleaned = _strip_line(raw)
97	        if cleaned is not None:
98	            out_lines.append(cleaned)
99	    return "".join(out_lines)
100	
101	
102	def main() -> int:
103	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
104	    ap.add_argument(
105	        "path",
106	        help="File path or '-' for stdin",
107	    )
108	    args = ap.parse_args()
109	
110	    if args.path == "-":
111	        text = sys.stdin.read()
112	    else:
113	        text = Path(args.path).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
114	
115	    sys.stdout.write(strip_text(text))
116	    return 0
117	
118	
119	if __name__ == "__main__":
120	    raise SystemExit(main())
121
